title: Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple
tagline: Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

heroImage.alt: Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple, Kerala

overview.short: Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple is located at Nayathod, a small village in Ernakulam District, Kerala, India.
overview.description: Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple is located at Nayathod, a small village in Ernakulam District, Kerala, India. The temple is located at about 3 km from Cochin International Airport. This temple is a protected monument by Archeological Department, Kerala. The deities at this temple are Lord Shiva and Lord Vishnu.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: CIAL Solar Power Project
topPlaces.description: The CIAL Solar Power Project is a 50 megawatt (MW) photovoltaic power station built at Cochin International Airport, India, by the company Cochin International Airport Limited (CIAL). Cochin International Airport became the first fully solar powered airport in the world with the commissioning the plant.

topPlaces.name: Vadakkumbhagom
topPlaces.description: Vadakkumbhagom is a village in Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Mattoor
topPlaces.description: Mattoor is a small town in the Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is situated 2 km (1.2 mi) north of Kalady on MC Road.

topPlaces.name: Mar Hormizd Syro-Malabar Cathedral, Angamaly
topPlaces.description: Mar Hormizd Cathedral, locally known as the Eastern Church of Angamaly (Malayalam: അങ്കമാലി കിഴക്കേപ്പള്ളി, romanized: Angamali Kiḻakkeppaḷḷi) or the Cathedral Church (Malayalam: അരമനപള്ളി, romanized: Aramanappaḷḷi), is a Syro-Malabar church in Angamaly, India. It was built in 1578 by Mar Abraham, the last East Syriac Metropolitan to reach the Malabar Coast.

topPlaces.name: Athanasius Paulose II
topPlaces.description: Athanasius Paulose (27 June 1915 – 7 March 1991) was the First Metropolitan of Evangelistic Association of the East. He was consecrated as Metropolitan in 1973.

topPlaces.name: Eravikulangara Temple
topPlaces.description: Eravikulangara Bhagavathy Temple is an ancient Hindu temple in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India, dedicated to Bhagavan Sivan and Bhagavan Ayyappan.

topPlaces.name: Akaparambu
topPlaces.description: Akaparambu, or Akapparambu is a village in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India, near the Cochin International Airport and south of the town of Angamaly.

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Jacobite Soonoro Cathedral, Angamaly
topPlaces.description: St. Mary's Jacobite Syrian Soonoro Cathedral, or Angamaly Cheriyapally, is an ancient Jacobite Syrian church located in Angamaly.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (2 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (25 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are CIAL Solar Power Project, Vadakkumbhagom, Mattoor, Mar Hormizd Syro-Malabar Cathedral, Angamaly, Athanasius Paulose II.
